Title: Elucidation of the biosynthesis of carnosic acid and its reconstitution in yeast

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Ferruginol-forming branch from abietatriene; alternative branch toward pisiferic acid when combined with CYP76AK enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes abietatriene to ferruginol; has only trace capacity for further oxidation of ferruginol to 11-hydroxyferruginol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms key intermediate and can weakly contribute precursor toward carnosic acid; in combination with CYP76AK8 mainly diverts flux toward pisiferic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstitution; microsomal in vitro enzyme assay; GC-MS; LC-MS; NMR quantification; structure-guided mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in yeast under synthetic galactose-inducible promoters
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf surface trichomes/young leaves in rosemary and sage species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant food additive activity; anti-inflammatory; anti-tumour; antimicrobial
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ferruginol accumulation; with CYP76AK8 yields pisiferal and pisiferic acid, while only trace carnosic acid is formed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in yeast; greenhouse long-day growth for source plants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower efficiency toward carnosic acid than HFS enzymes; non-natural pairing with rosemary CYP76AK enzymes favors pisiferal accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering ferruginol or pisiferic acid branch, but inferior to HFS enzymes for carnosic acid production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH1 functions primarily as a ferruginol synthase converting abietatriene to ferruginol. 2. It produces only traces of 11-hydroxyferruginol, unlike CYP76AH22-24/CYP76AH4. 3. When co-expressed with CYP76AK enzymes, it supports production of pisiferal and pisiferic acid, showing ferruginol can enter the C20 oxidation branch. 4. Triple substitution D301E,N303S,V479F converts CYP76AH1 into an HFS-like enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The three residues D301, N303 and V479 explain lack of efficient ferruginol 11-hydroxylation relative to HFS proteins; CYP76AH1 was used as the FS reference for mutational conversion to HFS.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Engineered cytochrome P450 monooxygenase, CYP76 clan
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Engineered gene based on Danshen CYP76AH1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Engineered triple-mutant oxidizes abietatriene to ferruginol and efficiently hydroxylates ferruginol at C11 to form 11-hydroxyferruginol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of carnosic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; yeast co-expression assays; microsomal in vitro assay; GC-MS; LC-MS; NMR quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in yeast under synthetic galactose-inducible promoters
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recapitulates HFS activity with a metabolite profile indistinguishable from CYP76AH22, strongly increasing 11-hydroxyferruginol formation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Engineered enzyme; natural occurrence not established
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates that rational amino-acid editing can convert ferruginol synthases into carnosic-acid-pathway enzymes for metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Triple substitution D301E,N303S,V479F converted FS CYP76AH1 into an HFS-like enzyme. 2. The mutant showed GC-MS, LC-MS and in vitro assay profiles indistinguishable from CYP76AH22. 3. NMR showed an 11-hydroxyferruginol/ferruginol ratio of 18.12, nearly identical to native HFS.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is an engineered construct rather than a native gene, included because it is a primary experimental subject in Results and causally alters pathway output.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Rosemary or sage species-derived HFS used in study context; exact source stated as Rosmarinus officinalis or Salvia fruticosa among HFS set, CYP76AH22 source not explicitly resolved in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Carries out two successive oxidations, converting abietatriene to ferruginol and ferruginol to 11-hydroxyferruginol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of carnosic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstitution; microsomal in vitro assay; GC-MS; LC-MS; purification and NMR identification of 11-hydroxyferruginol; NMR quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in yeast under synthetic galactose-inducible promoters
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf surface trichomes/young leaves in rosemary and sage species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carnosic acid is a potent antioxidant food additive with anti-inflammatory and anti-tumour activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes 11-hydroxyferruginol accumulation and, with CYP76AK8, enables high carnosic acid production in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in yeast; long-day greenhouse growth for source plants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: 11-hydroxyferruginol can spontaneously auto-oxidize to hydroxyferruginol quinone
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key enzyme for engineering carnosic acid production platforms in yeast or plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH22, previously labeled ferruginol synthase, is actually an 11-hydroxyferruginol synthase. 2. It converts abietatriene to ferruginol and ferruginol to 11-hydroxyferruginol. 3. Co-expression with CYP76AK enzymes leads to carnosic acid formation in yeast. 4. In vitro coupled assays with CYP76AK8 showed complete conversion of 11-hydroxyferruginol to carnosic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reciprocal mutations E301D,S303N,F478V abolish ferruginol oxidation and restore FS-like behavior.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as HFS, carrying out successive oxidation from abietatriene to ferruginol and then to 11-hydroxyferruginol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of carnosic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstitution; GC-MS detection of 11-hydroxyferruginol
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in yeast under synthetic galactose-inducible promoters
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf surface extracts of rosemary/sage species contained the product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate HFS for pathway engineering of carnosic acid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH23 was reclassified from ferruginol synthase to 11-hydroxyferruginol synthase. 2. Expression in yeast led to the new product identified as 11-hydroxyferruginol.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Detailed source species not explicit in provided text.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as HFS, oxidizing ferruginol further to 11-hydroxyferruginol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of carnosic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstitution; GC-MS detection of 11-hydroxyferruginol
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in yeast under synthetic galactose-inducible promoters
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf surface extracts of rosemary/sage species contained the product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate HFS for pathway engineering of carnosic acid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH24 was reclassified from ferruginol synthase to 11-hydroxyferruginol synthase. 2. Expression in yeast led to production of 11-hydroxyferruginol.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Detailed source species not explicit in provided text.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Previously identified ferruginol synthase that also performs second hydroxylation to produce 11-hydroxyferruginol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of carnosic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstitution; GC-MS detection of 11-hydroxyferruginol; prior in vitro characterization cited
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in yeast under synthetic galactose-inducible promoters
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Intermediate toward carnosic acid and related antioxidant diterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: A mixed N-terminus/C-terminus sequence had to be used for yeast analysis because native amino terminus could not be amplified from cDNA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: HFS candidate for heterologous carnosic acid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH4 is not only a ferruginol synthase but also an 11-hydroxyferruginol synthase. 2. Expression in yeast yielded the new intermediate 11-hydroxyferruginol.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The analyzed construct used a chimeric sequence with CYP76AH22-derived N terminus and CYP76AH4-specific C terminus.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C20 oxidation module leading from 11-hydroxyferruginol to carnosic acid; can also oxidize ferruginol toward pisiferic acid and miltiradiene to miltiradien-20-al
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Carnosaldehyde/carnosic acid from 11-hydroxyferruginol; pisiferal/pisiferic acid from ferruginol; miltiradien-20-al from miltiradiene
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes sequential oxidations at C20, converting 11-hydroxyferruginol through aldehyde intermediate to carnosic acid; also oxidizes ferruginol and miltiradiene at C20 with lower efficiency
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms terminal product carnosic acid from immediate precursor through sequential oxidations
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal oxidation module; high-efficiency conversion of 11-hydroxyferruginol to carnosic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate selection from RNA-seq plus qRT-PCR; transient expression in N. benthamiana; yeast co-expression; GC-MS; LC-MS; NMR structural identification of miltiradien-20-al and carnosaldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Trichome-specific and enriched in young leaves relative to old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in yeast under synthetic galactose-inducible promoters and in N. benthamiana under 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Synthetic galactose-inducible promoters in yeast; 35S constitutive promoter in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of carnosaldehyde and carnosic acid with HFS; shows preference for 11-hydroxyferruginol over ferruginol
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young versus old leaves; trichome versus trichome-free leaf tissue; galactose induction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower efficiency on ferruginol gives pisiferal/pisiferic acid accumulation; non-enzymatic oxidation of carnosic acid to carnosol can occur downstream
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential terminal module for carnosic acid metabolic engineering in microbes or plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AK6 was identified from trichome/young-leaf enriched expression data as a candidate downstream CYP. 2. It oxidizes miltiradiene at C20 to miltiradien-20-al. 3. Together with HFS, it produces carnosaldehyde and carnosic acid. 4. It can also oxidize ferruginol toward pisiferal/pisiferic acid but prefers 11-hydroxyferruginol.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Defined as part of CYP76AK6-8 C20Ox group.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes diterpene substrates at C20; in transient assays with CPS and MiS generated miltiradien-20-al, and in full pathway context contributes to carnosic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms terminal product carnosic acid from immediate precursor through sequential oxidations
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; yeast co-expression; GC-MS; LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Trichome-specific and enriched in young leaves relative to old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in yeast under synthetic galactose-inducible promoters and in N. benthamiana under 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Synthetic galactose-inducible promoters in yeast; 35S constitutive promoter in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports C20 oxidation steps toward carnosic acid and intermediate aldehydes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young versus old leaves; trichome specificity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can also act on ferruginol and miltiradiene, reflecting broader substrate scope
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful terminal oxidation gene for reconstituting carnosic acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AK7 is trichome- and young-leaf enriched in rosemary. 2. Transient expression with upstream enzymes generated miltiradien-20-al, establishing C20 oxidation activity. 3. As part of the CYP76AK group, it supports downstream oxidation toward carnosic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This enzyme was the first CYP76AK tested by transient expression in N. benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C20 oxidation module leading to carnosic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Carnosic acid; carnosaldehyde; pisiferal; pisiferic acid; miltiradien-20-al
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes three sequential C20 oxidations from 11-hydroxyferruginol to carnosic acid via aldehyde intermediate; also oxidizes ferruginol and miltiradiene at C20
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms terminal product carnosic acid from immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: High-efficiency terminal oxidation step/module for carnosic acid production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; yeast co-expression; coupled microsomal in vitro assays with CYP76AH22; GC-MS; LC-MS; NMR quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Trichome-specific and enriched in young leaves relative to old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in yeast under synthetic galactose-inducible promoters
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: With CYP76AH22 yields robust carnosic acid accumulation in yeast; with CYP76AH1 instead mainly yields pisiferal/pisiferic acid
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young versus old leaves; trichome specificity; galactose induction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad substrate range can divert flux to pisiferic acid branch when paired with FS instead of HFS; downstream carnosol formation is non-enzymatic
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime terminal oxidation enzyme for constructing efficient carnosic acid production systems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AK8 belongs to the trichome-enriched CYP76AK C20 oxidase group. 2. In coupled in vitro assays with CYP76AH22 microsomes, it converted 11-hydroxyferruginol completely to carnosic acid. 3. In yeast, CYP76AH22 plus CYP76AK8 produced 8.26 μmol l-1 carnosic acid, the highest quantified pathway product. 4. With CYP76AH1, it instead yielded pisiferal and pisiferic acid, showing strong substrate preference for 11-hydroxyferruginol over ferruginol.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly states CYP76AK6-8 catalyze the three sequential C20 oxidations from 11-hydroxyferruginol to carnosic acid.
